diagrams.title: RSA Encryption/Decryption Process
diagrams.description: Shows how RSA uses modular exponentiation for encryption (c = m^e mod n) and decryption (m = c^d mod n)
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the mathematical foundation of RSA and how the public and private keys are related

diagrams.title: Man-in-the-Middle Attack on Diffie-Hellman
diagrams.description: Shows Eve intercepting and establishing separate key exchanges with Alice and Bob
elements: Eve intercepts, sends g^x to Alice
Eve has keys g^ax and g^by
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how MITM attack works and why authentication is essential

diagrams.title: Timing Attack Process on RSA
diagrams.description: Shows how timing variations during modular exponentiation can leak private key information
elements: Measure decryption time for different ciphertexts
Analyze correlation between time and key bit
Deduce each bit of private exponent d
Build complete private key from bits
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how side-channel information can compromise even mathematically secure algorithms
